title: alyssum montanum var. atlanticum
intro: Alyssum montanum var. atlanticum, also known as mountain alyssum or sea alyssum, is a low-growing, flowering plant native to the coastal regions of Europe. It belongs to the Brassicaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant's health benefits include its ability to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and act as an expectorant to relieve coughs and congestion. Alyssum montanum var. atlanticum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
